steps.title: Spot the confirmation pattern
steps.content: The first sentence claims the installation creates a “whimsical yet perplexing experience.” The second sentence shows this effect in action: visitors are “dazzled and confused.” The transition word must signal that this proves the claim.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Indeed introduces evidence that confirms a claim—the visitor experience proves the artist's success. Nevertheless, Instead, and Second suggest contrast, replacement, or enumeration, not confirmation. This matches choice C — C is correct.
